# Test cases
rho1 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, 0]])  # Pure |0⟩
rho2 = np.eye(2) / 2  # Mixed
rho3 = np.array([[0.8, 0.2], [0.2, 0.2]])  # General

exercises.solution:
import numpy as np
from scipy import linalg

def quantum_relative_entropy(rho, sigma, tol=1e-12):
    """
    Calculate D(ρ || σ) = Tr(ρ log ρ) - Tr(ρ log σ).
    """
    rho = np.array(rho)
    sigma = np.array(sigma)
    
    # Eigendecomposition
    eig_rho, U_rho = np.linalg.eigh(rho)
    eig_sigma, U_sigma = np.linalg.eigh(sigma)
    
    # Check support condition: supp(ρ) ⊆ supp(σ)
    for i, lam in enumerate(eig_rho):
        if lam > tol:
            vec = U_rho[:, i]
            # Project onto sigma's null space
            null_mask = eig_sigma < tol
            if np.any(null_mask):
                null_space = U_sigma[:, null_mask]
                projection = np.sum(np.abs(null_space.conj().T @ vec)**2)
                if projection > tol:
                    return np.inf
    
    # Term 1: Tr(ρ log ρ)
    eig_rho_pos = eig_rho[eig_rho > tol]
    term1 = np.sum(eig_rho_pos * np.log2(eig_rho_pos))
    
    # Term 2: Tr(ρ log σ)
    # Compute log(σ) using eigendecomposition
    log_eig_sigma = np.where(eig_sigma > tol, 
                              np.log2(np.maximum(eig_sigma, tol)),
                              -1e15)  # Very negative for null space
    log_sigma = U_sigma @ np.diag(log_eig_sigma) @ U_sigma.conj().T
    term2 = np.real(np.trace(rho @ log_sigma))
    
    return term1 - term2

def verify_klein_inequality(rho, sigma):
    """Verify D(ρ || σ) ≥ 0."""
    D = quantum_relative_entropy(rho, sigma)
    return D >= -1e-10

# Test
print("Quantum Relative Entropy Tests")
print("="*50)

states = [
    ('Pure |0⟩', np.array([[1, 0], [0, 0]])),
    ('Pure |1⟩', np.array([[0, 0], [0, 1]])),
    ('Mixed I/2', np.eye(2) / 2),
    ('Partial', np.array([[0.7, 0], [0, 0.3]]))
]

print("D(ρ || σ) matrix:")
print(f"{'':>15}", end='')
for name, _ in states:
    print(f"{name:>12}", end='')
print()

for name_r, rho in states:
    print(f"{name_r:>15}", end='')
    for name_s, sigma in states:
        D = quantum_relative_entropy(rho, sigma)
        if D == np.inf:
            print(f"{'∞':>12}", end='')
        else:
            print(f"{D:>12.4f}", end='')
    print()

# Verify Klein's inequality
print("\nKlein's Inequality Verification:")
for name_r, rho in states:
    for name_s, sigma in states:
        satisfied = verify_klein_inequality(rho, sigma)
        D = quantum_relative_entropy(rho, sigma)
        if not satisfied and D != np.inf:
            print(f"VIOLATION: D({name_r} || {name_s}) = {D}")
print("All finite cases satisfy D ≥ 0 ✓")

exercises.title: Monotonicity Verification

exercises.starterCode:

# Test monotonicity
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def quantum_relative_entropy(rho, sigma, tol=1e-12):
    # (Previous implementation)
    eig_rho, U_rho = np.linalg.eigh(rho)
    eig_sigma, U_sigma = np.linalg.eigh(sigma)
    
    for i, lam in enumerate(eig_rho):
        if lam > tol:
            vec = U_rho[:, i]
            null_mask = eig_sigma < tol
            if np.any(null_mask):
                null_space = U_sigma[:, null_mask]
                projection = np.sum(np.abs(null_space.conj().T @ vec)**2)
                if projection > tol:
                    return np.inf
    
    eig_rho_pos = eig_rho[eig_rho > tol]
    term1 = np.sum(eig_rho_pos * np.log2(eig_rho_pos))
    
    log_eig_sigma = np.where(eig_sigma > tol, np.log2(np.maximum(eig_sigma, tol)), -1e15)
    log_sigma = U_sigma @ np.diag(log_eig_sigma) @ U_sigma.conj().T
    term2 = np.real(np.trace(rho @ log_sigma))
    
    return term1 - term2

def apply_depolarizing_channel(rho, p):
    """Depolarizing: ρ → (1-p)ρ + p·I/d"""
    d = rho.shape[0]
    return (1-p) * rho + p * np.eye(d) / d

def apply_amplitude_damping(rho, gamma):
    """Amplitude damping channel for qubit."""
    K0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1-gamma)]])
    K1 = np.array([[0, np.sqrt(gamma)], [0, 0]])
    return K0 @ rho @ K0.conj().T + K1 @ rho @ K1.conj().T

def apply_phase_damping(rho, gamma):
    """Phase damping (dephasing) channel."""
    K0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1-gamma)]])
    K1 = np.array([[0, 0], [0, np.sqrt(gamma)]])
    return K0 @ rho @ K0.conj().T + K1 @ rho @ K1.conj().T

def verify_monotonicity(rho, sigma, channel_fn, channel_name):
    """Verify D(E(ρ)||E(σ)) ≤ D(ρ||σ)."""
    D_before = quantum_relative_entropy(rho, sigma)
    
    rho_after = channel_fn(rho)
    sigma_after = channel_fn(sigma)
    D_after = quantum_relative_entropy(rho_after, sigma_after)
    
    if D_before == np.inf:
        satisfied = True  # D_after ≤ ∞ always
    else:
        satisfied = D_after <= D_before + 1e-10
    
    return D_before, D_after, satisfied

# Test
print("Monotonicity of Relative Entropy")
print("="*60)

rho = np.array([[0.8, 0.2], [0.2, 0.2]])
sigma = np.array([[0.5, 0.1], [0.1, 0.5]])

channels = [
    ('Depolarizing (p=0.1)', lambda r: apply_depolarizing_channel(r, 0.1)),
    ('Depolarizing (p=0.5)', lambda r: apply_depolarizing_channel(r, 0.5)),
    ('Amplitude Damping (γ=0.2)', lambda r: apply_amplitude_damping(r, 0.2)),
    ('Phase Damping (γ=0.3)', lambda r: apply_phase_damping(r, 0.3)),
]

print(f"Initial D(ρ||σ) = {quantum_relative_entropy(rho, sigma):.4f}")
print()
print(f"{'Channel':<30} | {'D_before':>10} | {'D_after':>10} | {'Monotone?':>10}")
print("-"*65)

for name, channel_fn in channels:
    D_before, D_after, satisfied = verify_monotonicity(rho, sigma, channel_fn, name)
    status = '✓' if satisfied else '✗'
    print(f"{name:<30} | {D_before:>10.4f} | {D_after:>10.4f} | {status:>10}")

print("\nAll channels satisfy monotonicity ✓")

testCases.expected: D always decreases or stays same

exercises.title: Pinsker's Inequality
exercises.description: Verify Pinsker's inequality numerically for various quantum states and plot the relationship between relative entropy and trace distance.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def trace_distance(rho, sigma):
    """Calculate trace distance ||ρ - σ||_1 / 2."""
    # Your code here
    pass

def verify_pinsker(rho, sigma):
    """
    Verify: D(ρ||σ) ≥ (1/2ln2) ||ρ-σ||_1²
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def plot_pinsker_bound():
    """Plot relative entropy vs trace distance."""
    # Your code here
    pass

plot_pinsker_bound()
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def quantum_relative_entropy(rho, sigma, tol=1e-12):
    # (Previous implementation)
    eig_rho, U_rho = np.linalg.eigh(rho)
    eig_sigma, U_sigma = np.linalg.eigh(sigma)
    
    for i, lam in enumerate(eig_rho):
        if lam > tol:
            vec = U_rho[:, i]
            null_mask = eig_sigma < tol
            if np.any(null_mask):
                null_space = U_sigma[:, null_mask]
                projection = np.sum(np.abs(null_space.conj().T @ vec)**2)
                if projection > tol:
                    return np.inf
    
    eig_rho_pos = eig_rho[eig_rho > tol]
    term1 = np.sum(eig_rho_pos * np.log2(eig_rho_pos)) if len(eig_rho_pos) > 0 else 0
    
    log_eig_sigma = np.where(eig_sigma > tol, np.log2(np.maximum(eig_sigma, tol)), -1e15)
    log_sigma = U_sigma @ np.diag(log_eig_sigma) @ U_sigma.conj().T
    term2 = np.real(np.trace(rho @ log_sigma))
    
    return term1 - term2

def trace_distance(rho, sigma):
    """Calculate trace distance ||ρ - σ||_1 / 2."""
    diff = rho - sigma
    eigenvalues = np.linalg.eigvalsh(diff)
    return 0.5 * np.sum(np.abs(eigenvalues))

def trace_norm(rho, sigma):
    """Calculate trace norm ||ρ - σ||_1."""
    diff = rho - sigma
    eigenvalues = np.linalg.eigvalsh(diff)
    return np.sum(np.abs(eigenvalues))

def verify_pinsker(rho, sigma):
    """Verify Pinsker's inequality."""
    D = quantum_relative_entropy(rho, sigma)
    T1 = trace_norm(rho, sigma)
    
    if D == np.inf:
        return True, np.inf, T1
    
    pinsker_bound = T1**2 / (2 * np.log(2))  # Using natural log
    satisfied = D >= pinsker_bound - 1e-10
    
    return satisfied, D, pinsker_bound

def plot_pinsker_bound():
    """Plot relative entropy vs trace distance."""
    np.random.seed(42)
    
    results = []
    
    # Generate random pairs of states
    for _ in range(200):
        # Random qubit states
        a = np.random.random(2) + 1j * np.random.random(2)
        a /= np.linalg.norm(a)
        psi = a
        rho = np.outer(psi, psi.conj())
        
        b = np.random.random(2) + 1j * np.random.random(2)
        b /= np.linalg.norm(b)
        phi = b
        sigma = np.outer(phi, phi.conj())
        
        # Mix with maximally mixed
        p = np.random.random()
        rho = p * rho + (1-p) * np.eye(2) / 2
        
        q = np.random.random()
        sigma = q * sigma + (1-q) * np.eye(2) / 2
        
        D = quantum_relative_entropy(rho, sigma)
        T = trace_norm(rho, sigma)
        
        if D != np.inf and D < 10:  # Filter outliers
            results.append((T, D))
    
    T_vals, D_vals = zip(*results)
    
    # Pinsker bound curve
    T_curve = np.linspace(0, 2, 100)
    pinsker_curve = T_curve**2 / (2 * np.log(2))
    
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
    plt.scatter(T_vals, D_vals, alpha=0.5, s=20, label='Random state pairs')
    plt.plot(T_curve, pinsker_curve, 'r-', linewidth=2, label='Pinsker bound: D ≥ T₁²/(2ln2)')
    plt.xlabel('Trace Norm ||ρ - σ||₁', fontsize=12)
    plt.ylabel('Relative Entropy D(ρ || σ)', fontsize=12)
    plt.title("Pinsker's Inequality", fontsize=14)
    plt.legend()
    plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    plt.xlim(0, 2)
    plt.ylim(0, max(D_vals) * 1.1)
    plt.show()
    
    # Verify all points
    violations = sum(1 for T, D in results if D < T**2 / (2 * np.log(2)) - 1e-10)
    print(f"\nPinsker's inequality verified: {len(results) - violations}/{len(results)} points satisfy")
    if violations > 0:
        print(f"WARNING: {violations} apparent violations (likely numerical)")
    else:
        print("All points above the Pinsker bound ✓")

plot_pinsker_bound()
hints: Trace norm = sum of absolute eigenvalues of difference
